_comment: Reference config for OpenClaw 2026.3.1. The actual config is generated via CLI (openclaw config set, openclaw agents add). This file documents the target state.

mappings.messageTemplate: Notion webhook received. Event type: {{type}}. Ticket data: {{json}}. Read the full ticket from Notion, resolve the repository from the Project Registry, assess ticket clarity, and route to the appropriate pipeline (refine for vague tickets, feature for clear ones, hotfix for critical bugs).
GitHub PR webhook received. Action: {{action}}. PR #{{pull_request.number}} on {{repository.full_name}}. Title: {{pull_request.title}}. Dispatch a review pipeline if appropriate.
PR #{{pull_request.number}} review completed. If all checks pass, call the Dispatch Service /dispatch/qa endpoint to trigger QA pipeline.

Dispatch Service callback: event={{event}}, pipeline={{data.pipeline}}, status={{data.status}}, ticketId={{data.ticketId}}, prUrl={{data.prUrl}}, prNumber={{data.prNumber}}, branch={{data.branch}}, repository={{data.repository}}, cost={{data.costUsd}}USD, duration={{data.durationMs}}ms, session={{data.sessionId}}, summary={{data.summary}}. If pipeline=refine, parse the summary JSON and act on the refine result (pass_through → dispatch feature, decompose → create sub-tickets and dispatch each, escalate → add questions to Notion). Otherwise, follow the pipeline chaining logic in BOOTSTRAP.md: feature/hotfix with PR → dispatch review; review approved → dispatch QA; QA passed → mark done; failure → mark failed.
